summary: Retire the built-in `spec` kind (`src/spec.rs`, `extract::spec_features`, `Workspace.specs`, `discover_spec_files`, drift's spec axis) â€” the data-declared `spec` kind now covers it.

per.section: Decision: a custom kind is declared data, never engine code

edit.description: Remove `Workspace.specs`, the `Spec::from_surface_dir` load in `Workspace::load`, `WorkspaceError::Spec`, `use crate::spec`, the `write_surface_spec` test helper, and the two spec-load tests (`load_reconstructs_specs_sorted_alongside_skills_and_rules`, `load_of_a_workspace_without_specs_dir_is_empty`); drop the `specs:` field from the remaining `Workspace { .. }` test literals. Surface loading keeps only the built-in `skills`/`rules`; custom-unit reading is generic (KIND-CUSTOM-UNIT-LOADER's `Unit::from_surface_dir`), not spec-special.

Drop `spec_features` (dead outside its test â€” `main.rs` extracts a custom kind via `custom.extraction.extract`, never `spec_features`) and its unit test `spec_features_expose_id_body_lines_headings_and_source_dir`, the `spec_in` test helper, and `use crate::spec::Spec`. A custom kind extracts via the data algebra, not a bespoke projector.

Remove the built-in spec drift axis: the `specs` block in `diff` (reads `workspace.specs` + `import::discover_spec_files`), the `"spec"` `DriftEntry` kind it emits, and the `DriftEntry.kind`/`added_name` doc comments that name `spec`. No spec-specific drift *test* exists (the drift tests use skill+rule fixtures), so none is removed. Generic custom-kind drift is future work alongside `apply`, not part of this retirement (scope cut, stated out loud).

Drop `discover_spec_files` (drift was its only caller). Reconcile the one import test that reloads the written spec surface â€” `writes_a_spec_surface_and_rollup_row`, which calls `crate::spec::Spec::from_surface_dir` â€” to the generic `crate::kind::Unit::from_surface_dir`, asserting `unit.id`/`unit.body` in place of `reloaded.name`/`reloaded.body`. Production import already writes custom units generically (`import_custom_unit`) and depends on no `Spec`. (There is no `import_spec`.)

tests.asserts: The data-declared `spec` custom-kind path (`check_dispatches_the_spec_custom_kind_through_its_extractor_and_contract`, `check_reads_a_custom_kind_rooted_outside_specs`) stays green with the built-in `Spec` IR, `spec_features`, `Workspace.specs`, drift's spec axis, and `discover_spec_files` all removed â€” `temper` reads `specs/` solely through the generic loader + composed extractor, and the whole suite stays green.

acceptance: The built-in `spec` module and its bespoke plumbing (the `Spec` IR, `spec_features`, `Workspace.specs`, `discover_spec_files`, drift's spec axis) are removed; `temper` reads `specs/` only through the data-declared custom kind via the generic loader; the whole suite is green.
notes: KIND-CUSTOM-UNIT-LOADER shipped (`Unit::from_surface_dir`), so this is now near-pure deletion; `main.rs` already reads custom units generically (no main.rs edit). `tests/cli.rs` is NOT in scope â€” its self-host import carries no root `temper.toml` so `specs/` is ignored, and `diff` runs over spec-less harnesses. Confirm no compile/test regression once `Spec` is gone (15-kinds Decision: temper ships no custom kind as a built-in).

summary: Reconcile the top-level `[[edge]]` construct into `[kind.<name>.relationships]` so a reference is a kind capability, not a separate construct.

per.section: The entity graph is a kind capability

edit.description: Move edge parsing from the standalone top-level `[[edge]]` array to `[kind.<name>.relationships]` under the owning kind (`specs/40-composition.md`, the `.relationships` surface) â€” the `<name>` is the edge source (the implicit `from`), and each relationship names its reference `field` and target `to` kind. Parse it for both a built-in-layer `[kind.<k>]` and a full custom-kind `[kind.<name>]` (relationships are orthogonal to the custom-vs-layer split). Preserve the parsed `Edge { field, from, to }` shape the graph consumes; drop `parse_edges`/`parse_edge`, `edge_str`, the `[[edge]]` reader, and `EdgeRootNotArray`/`BadEdge`. Update the compose in-module edge tests to the new shape.
Read the declared relationships off the owning kinds rather than a free-standing edge list; route resolution + admissibility behavior unchanged (still over `Edge { field, from, to }`).
Assemble the harness graph from the kinds' relationships instead of `AuthorLayer::edges()`.
Update the edge-construction fixture `ROUTES_TO_EDGE` and its `[[edge]]`-shaped assertions to the `[kind.<name>.relationships]` shape.
Assert relationships parse under `[kind.<name>.relationships]` (a new parse assertion alongside the existing `[kind.spec]` custom-kind ones).

tests.asserts: Edges declared under `[kind.<name>.relationships]` assemble the harness graph and drive route resolution + admissibility exactly as the standalone `[[edge]]` construct did.

acceptance: A reference is declared under its owning kind's `[kind.<name>.relationships]` (over that kind's declared reference syntax) rather than a standalone `[[edge]]` table; the harness graph, route resolution, and edge-admissibility behavior are preserved.
notes: Note (d) of the reconciliation. The graph foundation shipped soundly (`src/graph.rs`) over `AuthorLayer::edges`; this aligns the edge-declaration *shape* with the specced kind capability (15-kinds 'The entity graph is a kind capability'; 40-composition's `.relationships` surface). File-disjoint from the retirement but serialized behind it for review clarity. `degree`/`acyclic` and the spec kind's references-resolve clause read the same relationships and follow as later entries.
